title: Horizon Europe MAT-PROD-11: Innovative Technologies for Exploration & Data Modelling of Raw Materials
description: Research and innovation action for advanced exploration techniques and data-driven modelling to discover new deposits of critical raw materials in Europe. Supports AI/ML approaches, geophysical methods, and probabilistic geological modelling. Single-stage call under HORIZON-CL4-2026-01.
eligibility: EU-based consortia (minimum 3 entities from 3 EU member states). Non-EU partners can participate as associated partners. Core Element AI can join via EU consortium.
requirements: Consortium formation, technical proposal, work packages, budget, impact assessment, exploitation plan

title: Horizon Europe MAT-PROD-12: Technologies for Innovative Extraction of Critical Raw Materials
description: Research and innovation action for sustainable and efficient extraction technologies for critical raw materials. Covers mining optimization, AI-driven process control, environmental impact reduction. Single-stage call, TRL 3-5, Lump Sum funding.
eligibility: EU-based consortia (minimum 3 entities from 3 EU member states). Non-EU partners welcome. Core Element AI can join via EU consortium partner.
requirements: Consortium formation, technical proposal, work packages, budget, sustainability assessment

title: O'Shaughnessy Fellowships & Grants 2026
description: Equity-free funding for builders, researchers, and creatives working on ambitious, world-changing ideas. 10 Fellowships of $100K + up to 20 Grants of $10K+. Fully remote, open worldwide. No company formation required. Focus on demonstrated ability and vision over credentials.
eligibility: Adults worldwide regardless of background, education, or nationality. No credentials required. Every applicant considered for both $100K Fellowship and $10K+ Grant.
requirements: Application via forms.osv.llc/fellowships2026. Rolling review within 30 days. Stage 2 calls Feb-May, final selections by June 1.

title: EIC Accelerator 2026 - Open & Challenges
description: Europe's most competitive deep-tech funding. Up to EUR 2.5M grant + EUR 10M equity. 2026 has 6 batching dates. Step 1 (short proposal) rolling with 4-week evaluation. Reduced application from 50 to 20 pages. Challenges include critical raw materials supply chain (EUR 50M) and deep tech for climate adaptation (EUR 50M).
deadline_notes: Step 1 rolling. Step 2 batching: May 6, Jul 8, Sep 2, Nov 4, 2026
eligibility: EU-incorporated SMEs/startups or single researchers/innovators founding EU company. Non-EU companies must establish EU entity. Challenge: 'Strengthening European critical raw materials supply chain' (EUR 50M) highly relevant.
requirements: Step 1: short proposal (rolling). Step 2: full proposal 20 pages (batched). Step 3: interview. Simplified process for 2026.

title: DARPA I2O Office-Wide BAA (HR001126S0001)
description: DARPA's broadest entry point for AI researchers. The I2O focuses on AI, cybersecurity, autonomy, and information science. Supports proposals for novel AI methods including geoscience applications. Related: CriticalMAAS program transitioning to operational phase in FY2026.
eligibility: US entities. Abstracts required before full proposals. Individual awards $500K-$5M depending on scope.
requirements: Abstract submission, full technical proposal, budget, team qualifications

title: Emergent Ventures Fellowship & Grants
description: Low-overhead fellowship and grant program by Tyler Cowen supporting entrepreneurs with 'zero to one' moonshot ideas. Non-dilutive grants $1K-$50K. Rolling review with guaranteed response within 2-3 weeks. Global applicants welcome, 13+ years old.
eligibility: Open globally. Age 13+. No credentials required. Conversation with Tyler Cowen for shortlisted candidates.
requirements: Application via mercatus.tfaforms.net/5099527. Rolling basis, response in 2-3 weeks.

title: Foresight Institute AI for Safety & Science Nodes 2026
description: ~$3M total annual funding for AI applied to scientific frontiers in molecular nanotechnology. Grants typically $10K-$100K. Physical hubs in San Francisco and Berlin. Supports AI for design, simulation, construction, and assembly at the nanoscale.
eligibility: Researchers and innovators working on AI + scientific frontiers. Physical hubs in SF and Berlin.
requirements: Application via Foresight website. Review based on technical feasibility and world-changing potential.

title: EIT RawMaterials KAVA Call 13 - Upscaling
description: Accelerates launch of promising technologies in raw materials industry. Targets validated technologies needing testing, demonstration, PoC, and scaling before market. Covers mining, materials, and recycling. Requires consortium of 2+ partners from different EU countries.
eligibility: Consortia of 2+ partners from different Horizon Europe eligible countries, representing 2+ sides of knowledge triangle. 30% co-funding minimum.
requirements: Consortium, proposal, budget, TRL validation evidence

title: Innovate UK Sovereign AI - Proof of Concept
description: Development of Proof of Concept demonstrators of AI technologies with state-of-the-art performance in areas of strategic importance to UK. Grants GBP 50K-120K for short projects. Ensures frontier AI technologies are developed in the UK.
deadline_notes: Check exact deadline on IFS portal
eligibility: UK registered businesses. May require UK entity establishment. Share of up to GBP 1.6M total pot.
requirements: Application via Innovation Funding Service. PoC demonstrator plan.

title: DOE Battery Materials Processing and Manufacturing NOFO
description: Notice of Funding Opportunity for battery materials processing and domestic manufacturing. Part of ~$1B in planned DOE critical minerals NOFOs. Supports projects advancing critical mineral processing, battery-grade material production, and supply chain resilience.
eligibility: US entities: companies, universities, national labs, consortia. Small business set-asides available.
requirements: Full NOFO application when released. Pre-application: concept paper, team, budget estimate

title: ARPA-E RECOVER - Rare Earth and Critical Mineral Recovery
description: Program to develop transformative technologies for recovery of rare earth elements and critical minerals from unconventional sources including mine tailings, coal ash, and electronic waste.
eligibility: US entities including small businesses, universities, national labs.
requirements: Concept paper (when FOA opens), full technical proposal, budget, team
